hard_fail_conditions: Claims actual command execution, local file creation, page asset generation, source XML mutation verification, repository inspection, or private project inspection for the calibration fixture.
Presents planned or policy-required outputs as already executed outputs without execution evidence.
Uses or requests raw XML bodies, host-specific absolute paths, generated page XML payloads, _workspaces material, _workmeta raw truth, credentials, cookies, secrets, or private-state data.
Chooses 8 as the actual split page count instead of 11 Page boundary nodes.
Fails to identify Page as the page boundary node family.
Fails to preserve source page order and stable ordinal ids page_001 through page_011.
Treats titleblock Page Number or Page Count signals as complete reliable identity without warning.
Places page XML assets, calibration outputs, or derived page payloads under .workflow.
Performs or recommends normalization, semantic rewrites, material collection, PCB/MDD pairing, or connectivity inference during split.

Omits any required artifact family, or an equivalent clearly named section, covering boundary summary, split plan, manifest, index, provenance, role hints, readiness, downstream handoff, and open questions.
Routes downstream handoff anywhere other than page_xml_normalize_spec_v0.
must_have_requirements: Includes explicit profile metadata with model, reasoning_effort, species, and class.

Records calibration-specific observed facts, or explicitly equivalent structural evidence, including root element Design, one schematic, 11 Page nodes, max_depth 8, total_element_count 186608, source size 12670307 bytes, and streaming parser mode.

Records calibration-specific global structural counts, or explicitly equivalent scale facts, including PartInst 1067, NetScalar 1153, WireScalar 4378, OffPageConnector 538, PartInstUserProp 7644, IsNoConnect 3494.
Records the titleblock Page Count 8 conflict with the 11 observed Page nodes as a warning.
Records page number signals as missing/non-contiguous and therefore non-authoritative for split identity.
Builds an ordered split plan with page_001 through page_011 and project-local page asset refs.

Records per-page manifest/index facts for all 11 pages, at least stable id, source ordinal, non-sensitive payload-free artifact ref or planned ref, page role hint, and representative structural counts or warnings.
States source XML is read-only and unchanged; source mutation is disallowed.
States page-local XML payloads are preserved without normalization and raw payloads are not archived in public workflow canon.

Writes source provenance with source binding identity, sha256 prefix, parser mode, split method, page count, page order/id map, and a policy or planned check for source mutation depending on execution evidence.
Separates observed fixture facts, fixture-provided metadata, conservative inferences, planned outputs, policy constraints, and open questions.

Adds non-authoritative page role hints covering schematic content, hardware/material context where structurally signaled, possible PCB context where structurally signaled, offpage connector context where signaled, and unknown/review-required when ambiguous.

Split readiness is conditional when project-local output binding, actual page asset writing, or per-page checksums have not been executed; it still confirms policy containment, no .workflow page assets, no raw XML leak, no normalization/material collection/PCB-MDD overclaim, and readiness path for page_xml_normalize_spec_v0.
Open questions include the page count/page number mismatch and any need for project-local output binding or downstream review.
Keeps the answer compact enough to convert into YAML artifacts.

checks: no unnecessary detours or tool claims
